Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Hearing

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Matt Wells.
Dept. 514. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Brent M. Kernan, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Robert E. Thomas, who was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Matt Wells.
Plaintiff's first amended complaint was filed on August 9, 2016.
Order for Publication of Summons as to defendant Matt Wells was filed on April 6, 2017.
Plaintiff's first amended complaint was served via publication on June 13, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant on June 13, 2017.
Summary Judgment in favor of defendants Kenneth Ferro and Stephens Institute, dba Academy of Art University was filed on April 17, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on June 21, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Richard E. Thomas, and against the Defaulted Defendant Matt Wells, as follows:

Damages - $74,370.97.
Prejudgment Interest - $40,160.28.
Costs - $2,133.25.

Total - $116,664.50.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Brent M. Kernan of the law offices of Brent M. Kernan (510-666-6122) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorneys, Michael J. Vartain and Stacey L. Leask of the law offices of Vartain Law Group (415-391-1155) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS KENNETH FERRO, STEPHENS INSTITUTE DBA ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Or Summary Adjudication

Defendants Kenneth Ferro's and Stephens Institute's motion for summary judgement is GRANTED. Defendants satisfied their summary judgment burden by presenting evidence that plaintiff Richard Thomas is unable to prove either of his claims for intentional tort (trespass to real property) or negligence and plaintiff Richard Thomas has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue as to either of his claims. Strictly construing defendants' evidence and liberally construing Mr. Thomas' evidence fails to show any evidence from which would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find that Mr. Ferro entered Mr. Thomas's property or directed defendant Matt Wells to enter the property. Based on the evidence on this motion, neither Mr. Ferro nor the Academy of Art University owed a duty of care to protect Mr. Thomas' real property from harm by adult students. There was no special relationship between either of the moving defendants and Mr. Thomas or between either of the moving defendants and their adult students. Nor did moving defendants have any prior knowledge of similar incidents in the past. Per the doctrine of negligent undertaking, "the scope of any duty assumed depends upon the nature of the undertaking." (Delgado v. Trax Bar & Grill (2005) 36 Cal.4th 224, 249). Mr. Ferro's decision to instruct his students to protect public property and to seek permission before filming on private property does not show that either of the moving defendants assumed responsibility to private property owners to protect them from trespass committed by adult students enrolled in Mr. Ferro's class. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: mtorreano@sftc.org (L&M302)
